toneVariations: Direct and factual: focus on specific features and technical capabilities
Problem-solution: identify pain point, then show how product solves it
Comparison-based: contrast with alternatives (bot farms vs verified data, centralized vs decentralized)
Use-case driven: describe specific scenario where product shines
Stats-focused: mention concrete metrics (% engagement, # of KOLs, etc.)
structureVariations: Lead with use case, then technical detail
Lead with unique angle, then implementation
Lead with question/challenge, then answer with product
avoidRepetition: Don't start multiple replies with 'Agree on...' or 'Exactly...'
Vary product positioning: sometimes detailed feature, sometimes high-level platform
Rotate through different features of same product
Mix technical details with business benefits
Alternate between specific examples and general capabilities
